$comment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME (FC8 SUBSTRATE) end-to-end: a by-value COMPOUND-LITERAL aggregate rvalue `(struct S){…}` now has a runtime carrier (design B — a synthesized slot: alloca + init-into-slot + consume by address, the established memory-based aggregate model). The gap was GENERAL, not bit-field-specific: an aggregate ConstructAggregate rvalue had no lvalue address, so EVERY by-value compound literal (bit-field AND non-bit-field, arg/return/assign) fail-louded H0009 at lowerLvalueAddress. Covers a NON-bit-field struct literal in ARG + RETURN + ASSIGN-from-literal positions AND a BIT-FIELD struct literal in the same three positions (each packs per allocation unit via lowerBitfieldAggregateInitIntoSlot — a:3+b:5 share unit 0, plus a SIGNED s:4 that must sign-extend on read). ANTI-FOLD: every literal's operands derive from a mutable global g_seed loaded at runtime, so ConstFold/Mem2Reg cannot fold to a literal return — the slot materialization + per-field stores (and bit-field RMW packing) run at runtime in BOTH the baseline and optimized arms. g_seed=5: non-bf arg=12, ret=20, asn=20; bit-field arg=22, ret=8, asn=6; each verified against its runtime expectation, return ok?42:13. A wrong slot/offset, a clobbered bit-field neighbour, a mis-extracted field, or a missing sign-extension flips one equality -> 13. LAYOUT-RULE-AGNOSTIC: write+read share the gnu_packed rule, so the exit is correct on every target regardless of ABI-exactness (D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT). The optimizedPipelines arm proves the flow survives Mem2Reg/ConstFold (the baseline arm is the real-codegen memory witness). arm64 runs under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
